diagrams.title: Array Memory Layout
diagrams.description: Shows how a one-dimensional array is stored in contiguous memory locations
elements: Arrow from array name to first element
diagrams.purpose: Understanding contiguous memory storage and how indices map to addresses

diagrams.title: Pointer Arithmetic Visualization
diagrams.description: Demonstrates how pointer arithmetic works with different data types
elements: Shows ptr+n formula: new_address = current + n * sizeof(type)
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing how pointer arithmetic automatically scales by element size

diagrams.title: Array of Pointers vs Pointer to Array
elements: int *arr[3] - Three separate pointer boxes, each can point to integer arrays
int (*ptr)[3] - One pointer box pointing to array of 3 integers
Shows which parentheses matter and why
diagrams.purpose: Clarifying the crucial difference in declaration syntax due to operator precedence

diagrams.title: Dynamic 2D Array Allocation
diagrams.description: Shows step-by-step memory allocation for 3x4 matrix using pointer-to-pointer method
elements: First row: pointer array at heap addresses
Second level: each row allocated separately
Rows shown as contiguous blocks of 4 integers
Arrows from row pointer array to each row
Shows NULL termination not required for rows
Demonstrates how to access elements: arr[i][j]
diagrams.purpose: Understanding dynamic multi-dimensional array creation and memory structure

diagrams.title: Stack vs Heap Memory
diagrams.description: Shows memory layout of a C program with static, stack, and heap sections
elements: Stack for function calls and local variables
Stack grows down, heap grows up
Shows where arrays and pointers reside
diagrams.purpose: Understanding memory regions and why dynamic allocation is needed
